folio.label: 78v
folio.l: Haue here my trouthe , q¶ the knygút I gaunte
Than q¶ she , I dar me wel a vaunte
Thy lif is saf , for I wol stonde ther by
Vp on my lif , the Quene wol seye as I
Lat se , whicú is the pruddest , of hem alle
That weretú on , a kerchef or a calle
That dar sey nay , of that I shal the teche
Lat vs go fortú , witú outen lengere speche
Tho rovned she , a pistel in his eere
And bad hi~ to be glad , & haue no fere
¢ Whan they ben comen , to the court , this knygút
Seyde , he had hold his day , as he had higút
And redy was his answere , as he seide
Ful many a noble wyf , & many a mayde
And many a wydewe , for that they ben wyse
The Quene hire self , sittynge as Iustise
Assembled been , his answere for to here
And afterward , this knygút was bode appere
To euäy wygút , comaunded was silence
And that the knygút , shulde telle in audience
What thyngö , that worldly wommen , louen best
This knygút ne stod nat stille , as dotú a best
But to this questiouö , a noon answerede
W manly voys , that al the courte it herde
My liege lady , generally q¶ he
Wommen desiren , to han souäaignete
As wel ouer hire husbonde , as hire loue
And for to ben , in maistrie hi~ a boue
This is ©oure most desire , thougú ©e me kille
Dotú as ©ow list , I am here at ©oure wylle
¢ In al the courte , ne was there wyf ne mayde
Ne wydewe , that contraried that he sayde
But seiden he was worthy , to haue his lyf
And witú that word , vp stirt this olde wyf
Whicú that the knygút saw , sittyngö on the grene
Mäcy q¶ she , my souereyne lady Quene
Er that ©oure courte departe , do me rigút
I taugút this answere , vn to the knygút
For whicú he pligút me , his trouthe there
The first thyngö , that I wolde hi~ requere
He wolde it do , if it lay in his mygút
Byfore this courte , than preye I the Sire Knygút
Quod she , that æè me take vn to thy wyf
For wel thow wost , that I haue kept thy lif
If I sey fals , sey nay vp on thy fey
¢ This knygút answered , allas and weilawey
I wot rigútwel , æat swicú was myn byheste

note.notetxt: 1031The last two lines are crammed into the space of one line.ODR
